description_long: This object consists of three tall, slender trees with relatively thin trunks and a moderate spread of branches. The foliage is concentrated toward the upper portions, with leaves that appear to be simple and somewhat sparse, allowing light to pass through and creating visible gaps. The bark on the trunks is smooth and light-colored, while the leaves are a muted green, giving the trees a natural, slightly subdued appearance. The branching pattern is irregular, with branches emerging at various heights and angles, contributing to a realistic, organic silhouette. These trees are typical of those found in urban parks, along sidewalks, or in landscaped areas, providing shade and visual interest without overwhelming the space. No visible roots extend above the ground, and the overall form is upright and vertical.
description: A group of three slender, upright trees with thin trunks and sparse, muted green foliage concentrated at the top. The trees have irregular branching and a natural, subdued appearance.
description_view_0: Two of the trees are most prominent, showing their upper foliage and thin trunks, with the third tree partially obscured.
description_view_1: All three trees are clearly visible, displaying their full height and the spread of their branches, with the foliage appearing sparse.
description_view_2: The trees are seen from a different angle, highlighting the irregular branching and the gaps in the foliage.
description_view_3: The arrangement of the three trees is evident, with the tallest tree on the right and the others showing their slender trunks and sparse canopies.
